sources.claim: AI risk management is an explicit framework for incorporating trustworthiness into AI system design, development, use, and evaluation.
Provenance must be verified against expectations; unrecognized or unexpected provenance should not be silently accepted.
Provenance describes where, when, and how artifacts were produced so consumers can verify builds against expectations and rebuild if desired.
Architecture descriptions use decisions, concerns, correspondences, and correspondence rules to express and check relationships between architecture elements.

stage_interpretation: Export PASS and promotion PASS are different architecture states and must be represented by separate authority terms and correspondence rules.
